details.query: capital city of Australia authoritative source
details.intent: Find an authoritative source (Australian government site, Australian Parliament/Citizen portal, CIA World Factbook, or Britannica) that explicitly states the capital city of Australia so we can cite it.
target_facts: name of capital city of Australia
details.verification_steps: 1) Visit the top authoritative URL returned by the search. 2) Extract the sentence or phrase that explicitly names Australia's capital. 3) Record the page title and URL for citation.
details.why_reduces_uncertainty: Locating and visiting an authoritative source provides direct, citable evidence for the capital city rather than relying on memory.

action_result.query: capital city of Australia authoritative source
action_result.intent: Find an authoritative source (Australian government site, Australian Parliament/Citizen portal, CIA World Factbook, or Britannica) that explicitly states the capital city of Australia so we can cite it.
vector_context:
Plan extraction: define targets, likely authoritative sources, and verification steps

Target facts: (1) The name of Australia's capital city (expected: 'Canberra'). (2) One authoritative source URL that explicitly states the capital. Likely authoritative sources: - Official Australian Government site (https://www.australia.gov.au) or a government department page (e.g., Department of Infrastructure, Regional Development) - Australian Bureau of Statistics - The Australian Capital Territory government site (canberra.act.gov.au) - Reputable reference sources: Britannica (britannica.com), CIA World Factbook (cia.gov) Verification steps: 1) Search the web to locate authoritative pages that state the capital. 2) Visit the top government or other authoritative page(s) found. 3) Extract the capital city name, capture a supporting excerpt/sentence, and record the visited URL for citation. Measurable outputs: { capital_city: string, source_url: string, excerpt: string }. This plan reduces uncertainty by prioritizing official government sources and cross-checking with a reputable reference.

Merge: What is the capital city of Australia? Provide the answer and cite one authoritative source URL.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

results.title: Canberra - Wikipedia
results.description: The Australian federal government retains some influence over the ACT government. In the administrative sphere, most frequently this is through the actions of the National Capital Authority which is responsible for planning and development in areas of Canberra which are considered to be of national importance or which are central to Griffin's plan for the city...

results.title: Australia | History, Cities, Population, Capital, Map, & Facts | Britannica
results.description: Australia is the smallest continent and one of the largest countries on Earth, lying between the Pacific and Indian oceans in the Southern Hemisphere. Its capital city is Canberra, and its most important economic and cultural centers are Sydney ...

results.title: Australian states, territories and capital cities - Tourism Australia
results.description: Victoria (VIC) is the thriving heart of all things culture in Australia, centred in the state’s capital, Melbourne. The city has been the recipient of the ‘Most Liveable City in the World’ title multiple times, owing to its cultural diversity, modern cityscape and friendly atmosphere.

results.title: List of Australian capital cities - Wikipedia
results.description: There are eight capital cities in Australia, each of which functions as the seat of government for the state or territory in which it is located. One of these, Canberra, is also the national capital. Section 125 of the Constitution of Australia ...

results.title: Canberra | History, Map, Population, Climate, & Facts | Britannica
results.description: Canberra, federal capital of the Commonwealth of Australia. It occupies part of the Australian Capital Territory, in southern Australia, and is about 150 miles (240 km) southwest of Sydney.
